subject: PSYC
course_description: This course focuses on the development of a better understanding of the epidemiology, biology, health practices, and research methods involved in college substance use. Students will obtain a strong foundation in epidemiological practices using hands-on analytical sessions using real-world data.
course_notes: Pre-requisite (if any): QTM 100, PSYC 110 or 111; previous PSYC 223 recommended. Juniors and seniors ONLY. Permission of the instructor is required for enrollment. This course provides an immersive and intensive undergraduate research experience in applying regression methods with application to data on college substance use behaviors. The objectives for students are: 1. Develop an understanding of substance use in relation to behavioral health a. Describe how college/university environmental contexts impact health and risk behaviors b. Describe the epidemiology of substance use and abuse among college students c. Be able to test theories including campus-specific and broader sociocultural substance use risk and protective factors 2. Identify health-promoting behaviors for mediating stress and reducing risk behaviors for problem drug use. 3. Learn and apply basic epidemiological research methods to understand college substance use and associations with personality, mood, stress, and health behaviors. a. Learn to develop hypotheses using two or model independent and/or dependent variables b. Identify and apply appropriate statistical tests based on the nature of dependent and independent variables c. Interpret analytical models d. Learn how to design and present scientific presentations and a brief research report
